text.title: code-coverage Procedure
text.summary: Five-step procedure to bring a project to branch-coverage with diff-cover gate and mutation testing on critical modules.

list.item: Input: existing coverage config (`pyproject.toml [tool.coverage]` or `.coveragerc` or `jest.config.js`).
Action: enable branch coverage (`branch = True` for pytest-cov; `coverageThreshold.branches` for Jest).
Output: report now includes branch coverage column.
Action: run the test suite with coverage to produce `coverage.xml`. Record repo-total branch %.
Output: baseline number for the soft trend gate.
Action: add a CI step that runs `diff-cover coverage.xml --fail-under=90 --compare-branch=origin/main` after the coverage step. Use `templates/diff-cover-ci.sh`.
Output: PRs that drop diff coverage below 90% fail CI.
Input: list of modules where coverage % is misleading (auth, payments, RBAC).
Action: add a `critical: true` marker (per-module or in a config file). These get a mutation testing step.
Action: add a nightly (or weekly) CI step running `mutmut run` / `stryker run` on the critical modules. Surviving mutants fail the job.
Output: mutation_run_on_critical = true in the report.
